author: cms-L1TK
body:
#### PR description:

This contains several months of L1 track code development from the entire L1 track community, notably:

1) Bug fixes to digitization of the tracklet track reco step, to help fix poor z0 resolution of HYBRID_NEWKF algo.

2) New tracking algo HYBRID_SIM_DISPLACED. Like HYBRID_DISPLACED, this does displaced tracking. Both use the same rough displaced tracklet track-finding stage. But the former uses a floating point representation of the duplicate track & KF fit steps in the firmware, whereas the latter uses floating point code matching firmware from a few years ago. 

3) Track Quality flag now available for HYBRID_NEWKF algorithm.

4) Various fixes to HYBRID_NEWKF algo to better match firmware.

- Complete rewrite of stub processing in DTC emulation to match new firmware.
- Significant bug fixes & rewrite of KF fit in HYBRID_NEWKF algo, to match new firmware.

5) Clean up of Tracklet pattern reco code, removing modules that no longer exist in firmware, where these old modules have now been merged together into combined ones. This affects modules such as TrackletEngineDisplaced, TripletEngine, and TrackletCalculatorDisplaced.

6) Reduction of CPU use.

7) Add helix covariance matrix to TTTrack EDProduct, so L1 trigger group can check if any of its elements are useful enough to add to HW output data.

#### PR validation:

git CI checks have been run ( results in https://gitlab.cern.ch/cms-l1tk/cmssw_CI/-/pipelines/15291130 ) to check that the latest code gives expected results on ttbar+200PU MC for prompt tracking (with both HYBRID and HYBRID_NEWKF algorithms checked) and on displaced SUSY + 200PU MC for displaced tracking( with HYBRID_DISPLACED algorithm checked).

Performance of both prompt and displaced tracking has been checked in more detail by members of the Tracker BES SW group in recent weeks.

The main change in HYBRID & HYBRID_DISPLACED L1 track performance is that the percentage of duplicate tracks is ~4 times higher, as a result of implementing a duplicate track removal algo that is FPGA-friendlier. (These algos are currently used for L1 trigger group studies).

Several bugs & logic errors have been fixed in the HYBRID_NEWKF algorithm, which is the algo that corresponds exactly to the firmware. As a result, the very poor z0 resolution it had in the past is significantly closer to that of HYBRID.

#### Dependencies

This needs https://github.com/cms-data/L1Trigger-TrackFindingTracklet/pull/9 .

#### Target release

Any CMSSW 20 release. (Though as 20_0_0 will be used for the SIM-DIGI-L1-RAW step of major MC production compaign, I assume it will not go in that).
title: Updates to L1 tracking code from entire L1 track community
